classes.title: Variable Types
classes.description: Common and advanced variable types, acceced by AS

Long.description: A whole number between 0 and 2^32 (4.29 billion)

funcs.description: A series of letters, numbers and symbols

LongINT.description: A list of whole numbers between - (2 ^ 63) and 2 ^ 63 - 1

Single.description: A numeric floating point values that range from -3.4x10 ^ 38 and +3.4x10 ^ 38 with up to seven significant digits

Double.description: A numeric floating point values that range from -1.7x10 ^ 308 and +1.7x10 ^ 308 with up to 15 significant digits

classes.title: Exit Types
classes.description: This class contains all Exit Types

Sub.description: The program exit the current subroutine, Applies to Subroutines

Function.description: The program exit the current function, Applies to Functions

Do.description: The program exit the current Do-Loop, Applies to Do-Loops

For.description: The program exit the current For-Next, Applies to For-Next Loops

Repeat.description: The program exit the current Repeat-Loop, Applies to Repeat-Loop

classes.title: Global Defines
classes.description: This class contains all global #define Types

AD_Delay.description: The constant AD_Delay controls is the acquisition delay. This can be changed by adding the following constant.

ADSpeed.description: ADSpeed controls the source of the clock for the ADC module. It varies from one chip to another. InternalClock is a Microchip PIC microcontroller only option that will drive the ADC from an internal RC oscillator.

classes.title: Chips
classes.description: This class contains all supported chips

values.name: chip
values.funcdesc: The #chip directive is used to specify the chip model and frequency that Great Cow BASIC will use. The model is the specific microcontroller - examples are "16F819". The frequency is the frequency of the chip in MHz, and is required for the delay and PWM routines. The following constants simplify setting specific frequencies. 31k, 32.768K, 125k, 250k or 500k. Any of these constant can be used. As shown in the example below. If frequency is not present the compiler will select a frequency default frequency that should work for the microcontroller.

values.name: define
values.funcdesc:
#define will search through the program for Find, and replace it with the value given for Replace.
The compiler replaces a constant with the value, it then searches the line for constants again and will make any more replacements needed. It will do this up to 100 times on a line, then it will stop replacing and show an error. The limitation of 100 iteractions is to prevent something like "#define Const_A Const_B" and then "#define Const_B Const_A" from causing an infinite loop in the compiler.

values.name: config
values.funcdesc:
The #config directive is used to specify configuration options for the chip.
There is a detailed explanation of #config in the Configuration section of help.

values.name: include
values.funcdesc: #include tells Great Cow BASIC to open up another file, read all of the subroutines and constants from it, and then copy them into the current program.

values.name: dim
values.description: Dim variable[, variable2 [, variable3]] [As type] [Alias othervar [, othervar2]]
values.funcdesc: Available on all microcontrollers. The Dim variable command is used to instruct Great Cow BASIC to allocate variables or to create alternate names for existing variables (using Alias) or to create variables at a specific memory location (using At).

values.name: SPIMode
values.funcdesc:
Mode sets the mode of the SPI module within the microcontroller. These are the possible SPI Modes: 

MasterSlow| Master mode, SPI clock is 1/64 of the frequency of the microcontroller.
Master| Master mode, SPI clock is 1/16 of the frequency of the microcontroller.
MasterFast| Master mode, SPI clock is 1/4 of the frequency of the microcontroller.
Slave| Slave mode.
SlaveSS| Slave mode, with the Slave Select pin enabled.

SPIClockMode is an optional parameter to set the mode of the SPI clock mode. This optional parameter sets both the clock polarity and clock edge.

0| SPI_CPOL = 0 & SPI_CPHA = 0
1| SPI_CPOL = 0 & SPI_CPHA = 1
2| SPI_CPOL = 1 & SPI_CPHA = 0
3| SPI_CPOL = 1 & SPI_CPHA = 1

values.name: SPITransfer
values.funcdesc:
Available on Microchip PIC microcontrollers with Hardware SPI modules. 
 This command simultaneously sends and receives a byte of data using the SPI protocol. It behaves differently depending on whether the microcontroller has been set to act as a master or a slave. When operating as a master, SPITransfer will initiate a transfer. The data in tx will be sent to the slave, whilst the byte that is buffered in the slave will be read into rx. In slave mode, the SPITransfer command will pause the program until a transfer is initiated by the master. At this point, it will send the data in tx whilst reading the transmission from the master into the rx variable.

values.name: FastHWSPITransfer
values.funcdesc:
Available on Microchip PIC microcontrollers with Hardware SPI modules.
This command only sends a byte of data using the SPI protocol. This command only supports master mode.
As a master, FastHWSPITransfer will initiate a transfer. The data in tx will be sent to the slave.

values.name: Exit
values.description: This command will make the program exit the routine it is currently in, as it would if it came to the end of the routine. Applies to Subroutines, Functions, For-Next loops, Do-Loop loops and Repeat loops.
values.funcdesc: Exit Sub | Exit Function | Exit Do | Exit For | Exit Repeat
